I treated the prompt and prior log as a research record, not as proof. The prompt‚Äôs required format is the tame presentation with distinguished sigma,tau, implicit tau^sigma=tau^2, later generators in the pro-2 core, and exponents reduced modulo M=85667662080.The prior log‚Äôs only unrefuted candidate is still the dynamic tame-inertia word; the other candidates were killed by finite tame or index-two tests, and the remaining gap is the dyadic formation/arithmetic-action comparison rather than another abstract DemuŇ°kin check.

The live candidate remains:variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

Equivalently, with ŌÄ=ŌÄ2‚ąąZ and ő∑=2ŌÄ on odd tame inertia,r=xŌÉ‚ąíŌÄxŌÉŌÄx‚ąí1(xŌĄ‚ąíő∑)ŌÄ ((yŌĄ)ŌÄ)ŌÉy‚ąí1,where ŌÄ‚Č°40491355905,‚ąíŌÄ‚Č°45176306175,‚ąíő∑‚Č°50932630783(modM).The 2-primary coordinate of ‚ąíő∑ is irrelevant in this word, since that exponent is applied only to ŌĄ, whose finite tame images have odd order.I think two conventions should be frozen as equations, because the last several turns repeatedly printed reciprocal-looking formulas.For the sign-cover norm coefficient, with t the deck involution of kerőļ and with ŌĄ‚Ü¶őĪÓÄ†=1 a nontrivial odd tame-inertia character,Da=j=0‚ąĎŌÄ‚ąí1(tőĪa)j satisfies (1‚ąítőĪa)Da=1‚ąí(tőĪa)ŌÄ=1‚ąít.ThusDa=(1‚ąít)(1‚ąítőĪa)‚ąí1.

This is the direction that matters: 1‚ąítőĪa is a 2-adic unit, while 1‚ąít is radical. Hence Da is radical and ‚ąí1+D‚ąíő∑ is a unit, so the nontrivial odd tame-character blocks still contribute only unit Smith pivots.For the trivial tame-inertia block, with T the ŌÉ-cycle operator and s‚Č°ŌÄ(modf), the mod-2 linear relation is(1+T‚ąís)X+(1+T)Y=0.

When f=2a m with m odd, s‚Č°1(mod2a) and s‚Č°0(modm). For even f, s is odd, so 1+T‚ą£1+T‚ąís. The safe way to write the elimination is:(1+T)Ps (T)=1+T‚ąís,Y=Ps (T)X+YňČ.Then h(T)=1+Ps (T)+Ps(T‚ąí1)has no nontrivial f-th-root zero, because for ő∂f=1,ő∂ÓÄ†=1,(1+ő∂)h(ő∂)=ő∂‚ąís+ő∂s+1=ő∂‚ąís(1+ő∂2s+1),and gcd(f,2s+1)=1. This supports the prior nonsingularity calculation, but only with Ps defined by the displayed equation.My new contribution is a closed classification of which primitive first-IA anti-cyclotomic tests can occur at all.For even f=2n, the first mixed IA directions are indexed by the involutionsŌĄňČjŌÉňČn in the finite tame quotientőĒe,f=‚ü®ŌÉňČ,ŌĄňČ‚ą£ŌĄňČe=1,ŌÉňČf=1,ŌÉňČŌĄňČŌÉňČ‚ąí1=ŌĄňČ2‚ü©.

Their condition is(ŌĄňČjŌÉňČn)2=ŌĄňČ j(1+2n)=1,so the first-IA mixed residual depends only on d=gcd(e,2n+1),not on the complementary divisor of e. Thus a primitive exact-order c obstruction can first appear only when2n ‚Č°‚ąí1(modc).This gives a clean visibility criterion. Writec=i‚ąŹ‚Ąďiai,oi=ord‚Ąďiai(2).

Then there exists n with 2n ‚Č°‚ąí1(modc) if and only if every oi is even and all v2(oi) are equal. In that case n(c) is the least positive solution ofn‚Č°2oi (modoi)for¬†all¬†i,and the minimal primitive test is (e,f)=(c,2n(c)).

Proof sketch: locally, 2n ‚Č°‚ąí1(mod‚Ąďiai) forces n‚Č°oi/2(modoi), so oi must be even. The congruences n‚Č°oi/2(modoi) are compatible exactly when the 2-adic valuations of the oi ‚Äôs agree; the odd parts then match by the usual CRT divisibility condition.This changes how I would prioritize the remaining arithmetic comparisons. Sorted by degree ef=2cn(c), the first primitive exact-order anti-cyclotomic tests are:c359111713331927254365n(c)1235465991076f24610812101818201412ef62054110136156330342486500602780dimF2EcAc246101612201818204248

So, for example, c=15 is not a primitive first-IA test: ord3 (2)=2 and ord5 (2)=4 have different 2-adic valuations. Likewise, the earlier (91,12) obstruction is not primitive 91 at this layer; since 26+1=65,gcd(91,65)=13,so its first-IA mixed part is a c=13 component.This also means that the proposed (9,6) arithmetic comparison is logically downstream of the c=3 and c=5 primitive comparisons. Candidate-side residuals may have been normalized away in earlier turns, but the arithmetic-minus-candidate residual has not actually been computed for (3,2), (5,4), or (9,6). The clean next arithmetic program is therefore:c=3‚Üíc=5‚Üíc=9‚Üíc=11,‚Ä¶ unless one explicitly assumes the c=3 and c=5 arithmetic residuals vanish.For c=9, the coefficient test from the prior turns can now be interpreted as the primitive c=9 exact-order test. If

R(T)=r0+r1

T+‚čĮ+r8T8‚ąąF2 [T]/(T9‚ąí1)is the normalized arithmetic-minus-candidate residual, the primitive 9-part vanishes exactly whenr0=r3=r6,r1=r4=r7,r2=r5=r8.Only after that descent condition holds should one test the exact-order 3 and 1 components.So the live candidate remains quite strong, but the remaining proof obligation is still not cosmetic. The prior finite checks establish a candidate-side DemuŇ°kin and orientation package; they do not identify the actual arithmetic filtered tame action. The first-IA obstruction theory now has a precise primitive conductor decomposition, and the arithmetic residuals in those primitive components still have to be computed or killed by a formation theorem.

ESTABLISHED: The dynamic candidate remains the only unrefuted candidate. I fixed the stable equationsDa=(1‚ąít)(1‚ąítőĪa)‚ąí1,(1+T)Ps (T)=1+T‚ąís,and proved a visibility criterion for primitive first-IA anti-cyclotomic conductors: c appears iff the local orders ord‚Ąďa (2) are all even with the same 2-adic valuation. The minimal primitive test is (e,f)=(c,2n(c)).

GAP: No arithmetic-minus-candidate residual has yet been computed. The presentation is therefore not proved. The remaining missing ingredient is still a dyadic formation theorem, or explicit Kummer/Witt arithmetic showing that the true filtered tame action has zero residual in every primitive anti-cyclotomic component.

NEXT: Compute the normalized arithmetic residuals in order: first (3,2), then (5,4), then (9,6). For (9,6), test the primitive 9-component first using the coefficient equalities above, then descend to exact orders 3 and 1.[BREAKTHROUGH]
